reviews.review_text: We booked an accommodation over the weekend, and all I can say is it’s a wonderful place to get a rooms. it’s like 20+ minutes away from CBD, and our kids loved it during our stay there. They got small kiddie pool and a small playground. We were hesitant at first because of the reviews here in google, but all in all we are satisfied on our stay, the staffs are nice and accommodating. Will definitely visit again soon when we visit Sydney

My wife and I have stayed at this caravan park many times when visiting our family who now reside in Sydney. We absolutely love coming up from Melbourne, and one of the reasons is this great caravan park.
Even though you are so close to the metro train, which makes travel into Sydney so easy and accessible (we have a motorhome so no need to move it from the site!), you really feel like you are in the country miles from any big town.  This park is located in the Lane Cove National Park, and the flora and birdlife and walks from the park are brilliant. The amenities are also really well looked after each day.
However the best part is the staff and management.  They have always looked after us incredibly well, and been so friendly and professional.  In particular, we have got to know Sharon in reception very well. She is amazing, always so willing to accommodate all our needs, and her manner is always extremely courteous, genuine and warm.  We really love our chats with her!!
And the same qualities apply to everyone in the office that we have had dealings with.
No es de lo mejores discovery que estuve pero cumple .

Lovely setting and quite convenient for the city using the M1 Metro line.
Park is undergoing significant upgrades - and at the moment there is no power to any of the camping sites. (Amenities, camp kitchen and cabins all have power).
Great opportunity for us to stress test our upgraded solar system and take advantage of the $20 site offer!
This park is going to be amazing once all the work is completed. It’s a shame it doesn’t allow dogs as we will be unable to use it once we become dog owners again down the track.
Everything was good

This is the second time we have had the pleasure of staying here and the staff are super accommodating and helpful.
Today 7.05.26  our special circumstances of being stuck without a flight home due to flight cancellations they quickly sorted out a cabin for us

Thank you so very much - you are the best

Power issues at the park meant my booking could not be offered as paid 6 months prior. I understand issues happen but then to be treated as if that was my problem and not the parks was disappointing. Caused significant additional stress having to replan and move sites during our stay while being charged a premium price for the privilege in near empty accommodation. Provided feedback which was also ignored. Good location but poor customer service means I wouldn’t recommend or return.

Don’t stay away run away from this horrible place.  No power whatsoever and none expected until October 26’!  They knew they had no power due to some problem three days 3/20 prior to my booking for April, not only didn’t they inform me but charged me for a powered site over $100 a night.  When we arrived and found out  and requested a refund since it was too late to book elsewhere they reduced it to $80 a night! The four people next to us in the same rented campervan like ours, only two of us, were charged $30 a night after they complained about being charged $100 per night for a powered site. When we asked for the same we were told “dynamic pricing” and they changed their prices daily as they pleased. The family next to us arrived one day before us.  Bad  people, dishonest.

Rustige camping in het groen. Goede uitvalsbasis voor downtown Sydney.

Maar service is slecht. Langere tijd geen elektriciteit (jan tm nov 2026) en vendormachines accepteren wel het cash geld, maar geven vervolgens niet wat je bestelt. De receptie wijst je vervolgens door naar het tel. Nr. Op de vendor machine. Weinig eigenaarschap dus.

Reasonable park, at the moment powered sites are unpowered. Most people would require access to toilets around 7.30 am to 9am but for some reason they are cleaning at this time so obviously can’t use the toilets. I was told I could use the toilets in the other block and get the security code from reception, 8am, office is not open until 9am. What are we supposed to do do, this happens every day. Just not good enough, most caravan parks clean the amenities after 10am.
From booking to quality of cabins, location and setting this park is great

The experience was not so good for the price paid (Eastern holidays). They gave an upfront warning (a week ahead of booking done a month ahead) for electrical work necessary resulting in an unpowered site but promised a small refund (not given) if one opted to go ahead instead of full refund.
The site given was also sloping so needed levellers and unfortunately the water pressure at the site was also gone for a while during emergency repairs.
All luxury problems but the price was $158 for 2 nights...
No power at present on camp sites cottages have power it's a great place with exceptional staff has a camp kitchen and if U didn't no you were in Sydney City U could be anywhere the bush and such
Recht zentral und doch ruhig im Grünen gelegener kleinerer Campingplatz mit allem, was man braucht. Küche mit großem Kühlschrank, Mikrowelle, Spüle und Tischen mit Sitzen. Viele Sanitärräume.
Good Camground, close to the city

Safari tent Cabin is Amazing
Worth every $ paid
Beautiful bushland setting
My place is So spacious and clean luxury glamping style
A bit expensive but for a place to stay in Sydney its a good price. Kitchen was good, bathroom and showers were good. Service was good. Would stay again

Fijne camping met een goede locatie en treinverbinding met CBD Sydney. Alleen het receptie personeel was niet erg behulpzaam, zonde om dit contactmoment met je klant niet optimaal te benutten zou ik zeggen.
Fantastic park.

Stayed in a cabin for 2 nights - location is really nice amongst the trees and feels away from the hustle and bustle of Sydney.

The cabin though just felt grimy - like in need of a good, deep clean. There was black mould in the skylight in the bathroom, the door handles and light switches felt like there was a film on them and the cabin had a bit of a funny smell. I could go on but that sums up the vibe. The cabin was also extremely compact which was a bit average too.

Eine Frechheit!

Wir hatten monate vorraus diesen Platz reserviert und einen Tag vor Antritt wurde dieser einfach storniert, da es angeblich für 2Wochen keinen Strom gibt!!!!

Wir mussten mehrere Stunden investieren um einen Neue Platz zu organisieren. Es ist Wochenende und selbstverständlich ist alles Ausgebucht, was in der nähe vom Zentrum ist.

Nun sind wir ca. doppelt so weit weg von Sydney. Mit nur einem Wohnmobil sind wir auf die ÖV angewiesen, denn parkieren in der Stadt ist unmöglich. Uber kostet uns ein halbes Vermögen, aber Zug und Bus dauert so lange, dass dies keine Option für uns ist. Alles in Allem wird so Sidney, in Sachen Kosten, zu einem Alptraum.

Wir werden diesen Platz sicher nie mehr buchen, es schweige den weiter empfehlen!!!

Hier wäre KEIN- Stern angebracht.

Unfortunately, our stay was impacted by a noticeable cockroach issue in the accommodation, which affected overall cleanliness and comfort. In addition, we experienced unhelpful and unfriendly behavior from a member of staff when raising our concerns.

We stayed two nights at powered sites with 2 other families (kids aged 0-6) and loved our time there. Reception was friendly and happy to accommodate our request for our group to be close to each other. We liked that there was a flat platform to set up the tent, made it a little more comfortable.

The park was quiet which was both good and bad, us parents stayed up chatting and at around 10:40pm another camper came to tell us about the 10pm curfew for loud noises (didn’t think we were too loud…our kids were happy sleeping right beside us). Seems some people have taken up residence at the campsite.

The swimming pool was spacious and clean, the bush walks fun and accessible. Saw plenty pf kookaburras, water dragons, and cockatoos. The bathrooms were decent but could use an upgrade. Amenities were very convenient too — there was a fridge, gas stove, electric grill, sink, even a laundry area which we didn’t use. Shops are not far too in case you forget anything.

Check-in process was easy and convenient. We arrived after 6pm so were given a code to access the map and entry instructions. At check-out, we were all packed up before 10am but stayed a little later to take some group photos. Staff were nice and didn’t rush us out the gate.

Would stay here again, kids loved the pool in particular which was a little cold but perfect for cooling off after a bush walk on a hot day.
Caravan rv bus camping park

We had an amazing glamping experience at Discovery Parks – Lane Cove. The setup was beautiful, the atmosphere was peaceful, and everything was thoughtfully arranged to make the stay comfortable and memorable.

The only hiccup was a power outage during the night, which meant we had to sleep with the emergency light which we couldn’t turn it off. It was a little unexpected, but thankfully everything else about the experience was so great that it didn’t take away from our stay.

Overall, a wonderful getaway — we’d definitely come back!

Überall Kakerlaken, bei Beschwerde wurde uns gesagt sie lösen das Problem. Doch es wurde nicht wirklich etwas getan, die Kakerlaken wurden zwar ausgeräuchert aber nicht entsorgt also war alles voller toter Kakerlaken.
Bei ansprechen der Situation wurden wir
gebeten auszuchecken nun, müssen wir ein anderes airbnb suchen.
Nicht zu empfehlen.

The campsite was clean and located in the middle of a small forest.
The poolside was nice and offers places to relax.
But the Wi-Fi coverage at the campground was not good.
How parks should be

Nous sommes restés 28 jours dans ce camping, franchement il n'y a rien à dire, nous étions sur un powered site, proche des douches et toilettes qui etait très propre, proche des bbq area et de la kitchen Camp qui était très propre, la piscine également est très bien entretenue et les gens qui loge au camping sont en général respectueux, ça reflète bien l'ambiance de ce camping, le SEUL point négatif et ça malheureusement ce n'est pas de la faute du camping, ce sont les avions, étant en roof tent dès 8h du matin il y avait le passage des avions toutes les 15 minutes, mais franchement préféré ce camping à big4 lakeside qui est une horreurs et le Wi-Fi n'est pas très bon quand il y a beaucoup de monde.
Park is fairly well maintained and in a quiet area. Staff were friendly and helpful.

Gute Lage um nach Sydney zu kommen, der Platz für ein Zelt völlig ausreichend. Essensbereiche soweit ok. Die Duschen und Klos, naja das ist schon etwas versifft.

Aber ehrlich gesagt, soll da jemand jede Stunde die Waschbecken etc. abwischen? Nein.

Ein bisschen mehr Ordnung durch die NUTZER würde schon viel bringen…

Ansonsten Herd, BBQ, Spülmittel, Toaster, Kühlschrank, Waschmaschine, Trockner alles zur Gemeinschaftsnutzung da.
Das Personal war auch freundlich.

We stayed here for two nights. The check-in process went smoothly, but the location is very close to Sydney Airport it really felt like the planes were flying right overhead. They pass quite low, and you need earplugs if you want to sleep comfortably.

The showers and toilets could use some renovation. The shower head was rusty, and parts of the corners were falling apart. The property is close to North Ryde metro station, which is convenient, but overall it’s not a place we would choose to stay again for 70$ powered per night.

The park is a great location for being in nature and walking trails. Everything was nice except the cleanliness of the bathrooms. They need a new cleaner and a good, thorough clean !!
We stayed for over a week so I know, difference after each clean 🫥 (pretty much a wiped bench and toilet paper refill)
Speaking for the ladies amenities but I can’t imagine the men’s would be nicer.
Fantastic quiet spot for getting into Sydney on the metro. Brilliant facilities just needs more sun loungers around the pool

This place is a awful place for camping as everyone is literally squashed up next to each other with no space or privacy at all. When i arrived to check in at reception staff were very rude never greeted me and made me wait 10 minutes before attending to me. Then coming to find out the lady who checked me in placed me under a massive gumtree with huge branches that could fall anytime, and she knew there would be windy weather, And i only had a small tent for me and my child. Didnt want risk our safety so i ended up checking out early the next morning and losing 2 days  worth $80.. And not to mention to get in they give you a code to open the gates, literally had to get out of the car to put the code to open the gate, then get back in the car, and you have to do that everytime you go in or out or else you will be stuck there and the gate wont open... Awww and also Airplanes fly by in the early morning and day disturbing your peace.. if you are a camper avoid this place.

Accueil agréable, camping très propre que ce soit au coin cuisine, aux toilettes et douches.
Laverie correcte même si une machine est un peu vieillissante, tarif 4$ lavage et 3 $ sèche linge.
Emplacement camping car bien aménagé (eau + électricité), vidange noire fonctionnelle.
Piscine propre
Bien placé, le métro à 1,2km.

Great location, 15 minute walk to North Rhyde station which will take you straight in to the city. The bush setting is lovely and quiet, lots of wildlife, cockatoos, lorikeets and bush turkeys roam around and outside the gate we even spotted a monitor lizard.  Amenities were fine, cleaned regularly and showers were hot and strong. Washing machine was $4 per load, it didn’t rinse or spin the clothes very well.  Would stay again if in Sydney.  We paid $80 per night for a powered site.
The location and ambience were good; however, the cleanliness inside the cabin was poor. We found mould on the bed, and the floor was not properly cleaned.

Due to the bad weather we called reception and spoke with Sharon to request a change of dates. She was able to accommodate our request and happy to assist as we were not able to get to the Discovery Park due to flooding in the location we were staying in prior to our intended check-in date. She was professional, quick and polite and I appreciated her great customer service. Check in was smooth, quick and easy. Upon entering our accommodation (Deluxe Safari Tent) it was immaculate, spacious, family friendly and really clean and I was ready to give a rating of 5/5. Unfortunately, when getting the kids to bed we found brown stains on the bedsheets on one bed and on the other bed, the bedsheets had mold. Luckily we always take spare bedding so we used our own bedding instead. There was also a big dead cockroach in the box of teas, sugars and hot chocolates in the kitchen so that was also a bit of a put off. However, don't let this stop you from experiencing this place. It has beautiful surroundings, our experience with staff was great and the Deluxe Safari Tents are modern, new, spacious, clean and has a porch with a bbq. Wifi worked really well for us and the TV was set up with Netflix, Disney, Prime etc. so that was a bonus for the kids. Swimming pool could use heat as it's quite cold however that didn't stop us from enjoying a swim on the days we were there and hopefully next time we visit we'll try the Deluxe 2 bedroom cabins. Thank you for a great stay Discovery Parks - Lane Cove.

Perfect.
My new stay when coming to Sydney.
Close to Sydney CBD and situated a urban forest. You never realise that you are within stone throw away from the city. The Cabins are well maintained as well.

Emailed them on the 30/12/2025 and head office 12/1/2026 with no replies from either.. yet they can apparently answer Google reviews.

My husband, 2 children and I stayed at the Discovery Park in Lane Cove in December

Upon entering the cabin we noticed how it didn't look like it had been cleaned just the beds had been made. There was bits of food and general floor bits on the floor, on the table and even the bin cupboard still had bits in there.

The ceiling fans had heaps of dust and aircons were struggling and making noises upon which we realised the filters were over full. We asked for the aircon filters to be cleaned and after 5 days and 3 times reminders it was completed. We would have done it ourselves but I didn't want to get the filter dust etc on beds.

The bathroom was atrocious. Upon entering the shower I slipped on a slimey sludge and fell towards the wall hitting the shower handle which hurt and left a bruise. A massive build-up of shampoo/conditioner etc was there and you could actually see the build-up. Same thing in the basin, however no where near as bad. I could have asked housekeeping to come and clean it, however I figured if they couldn't clean the first time properly so I ended up doing it myself.

The cabin itself is nice but the cleanliness and lack of help from the staff is a huge let down.
We were really disappointed in our experience at this park due to the state of the cabin.

We also had issues with the laundry machines not working and overcharging our card multiple times and were told to call the laundromat people which we have called a few times and left messages with no calls back, I understand this is a problem direct with the laundromat people but we were told a few people had already complained so it wouldn't have been hard to pop up a sign to say they aren't working properly.
Fleksibel innsjekking, pris litt dyr, men ok i forhold til god beliggenhet. Kort t-bane tur til sentrum av Sydney☺️

We didn't get past the booking stage. 3 emails unanswered over a few weeks and I left 2 voicemails before I rang a third time and finally got through.
Requested 2 cabins, was told I need to email request (lmfao). After some back and forth about that (nothing wrong with the email address but they denied having received it) I requested the cabins with the parking bays down the side to accommodate our car and camper trailers. Told that they couldn't accommodate us.
Awesome service guys. Your location might win you lots of guests but your service and response times won't.

Awesome camping ground near a metro station in Sydney. It combines the convenience of a place in the city with the peace of a camping site in a small town. Full of friendly birds including cockatoos. The pool is nice as well.

The "Rec Room" has a giant, loud vending machine. It's the only indoor place to hang, which if you're camping could be a huge boon. Here it's like torture - the one slightly comfy spot has this electric monster yelling in the corner.
Hermoso lugar para ir con la familia

Stayed the night , for Sydney accommodation it was very affordable and comfortable cabin.

For park amenities the pool and playground were small, but Sydney has beaches and lots of playgrounds.

Would stay again for cheap Sydney accommodation, wouldn’t come looking for action packed park like the other discovery park locations.

Still very nice, room was set up well. Easy after hours check in and easy online booking process.

Would recommend

Booked and stayed for 4 nights over New Years, busy period. Booked out park. All the amenities were clean each time we went. Ample amenities spread throughout the park including communal cooking and wash up areas. They were able to shuffle our booking when our needs changed last minute, and they were very happy and super accommodating.
Close to the city but very calm and quiet place with a lot of trees and nature.
Excellent location.  Walking distance to national park and metro to central Sydney

we turned up last minute asking to stay the night - the girl at reception was super sweet and booked up in . We decided to stay an extra 3 nights and she booked us in no problem. The site was nice lots of bush turkeys and parrots ( I would recommend keeping ur snacks inside ur campervan as the turkey stole our chicken flavoured crisps ) the toilets/showers were not the nicest but were just okay.  There is a metro station about 10-15min walk away too that takes you straight into the centre of Sydney - the pool was nice too
Cabins in a lovely setting. Cabins are spacious and clean

This place needs a good make over. We arrived and set up our caravan next to a toilet block which was closed for repairs and was woken up the next day by the sound of a kango which went on all day so we asked to be moved which wasn’t straight forward. I don’t know why I was put on this spot in the first place as there was plenty of spaces available. The tv room was dirty and like a sauna with the drinks machine making so much noise it was impossible to hear the tv and I noticed there were no smoke alarms in there on a couple of occasions I witnessed people smoking in the tv room and showers.The showers were hit  and miss but mainly cold even though they had plumbers there working on them.. All in in all good location for the train but way over priced for what it is.

By far the rudest and most unprofessional staff we encountered during our lap of Australia. While the park itself is run down and poorly maintained, the dismissive and hostile attitudes of the reception staff and supervisor made an already poor experience significantly worse. The only real positive is the park’s proximity to the metro station.

We were allocated a caravan site directly beside an active construction zone and toilets that were out of order, with no warning given at check-in. The construction involved constant jackhammering, drilling, and heavy machinery operating from 6am through to 5pm. The noise was relentless, extremely loud, and completely unsuitable for anyone working remotely or travelling with children. I work remotely and homeschool my children, and the jackhammer noise made it impossible to hold work calls, concentrate, or complete online schooling. The Wi-Fi was also virtually nonexistent, compounding the disruption.

I went to reception first thing in the morning to politely request a move to a quieter site. Although the receptionist was superficially polite, she was unwilling to help in any meaningful way. She refused to check availability on the system, would not contact her supervisor, and insisted I return later—despite me explaining that I had an imminent conference call and that the jackhammering had already begun. Her tone and attitude made it clear that our situation was not a priority.

As a direct result of being left beside an active jackhammer construction site, I was forced to cancel my conference call and lost income for the day, and my children were unable to attend their online tutoring. When I returned later to speak with the supervisor, her attitude was appalling. There was no greeting, no apology, and no acknowledgement of the impact of placing paying guests next to loud jackhammer construction. When I explained the disruption and financial losses incurred, she responded by abruptly flinging a site map across the counter and turning back to her computer screen, refusing to engage further or even make eye contact.

The same dismissive behaviour continued when I raised safety concerns about the showers, which fluctuated between dangerously hot and freezing cold. A receptionist repeatedly stated “there’s no issues” in a blunt, confrontational manner, showing zero concern even after I explained that my children had nearly been scalded.

Overall, the staff—particularly the supervisor—displayed a complete lack of professionalism, empathy, or customer care. Being placed next to constant jackhammer noise without warning, combined with rude staff and poor maintenance, made our stay unnecessarily stressful.

Aside from the convenient location, this park is grossly overpriced. It appears the owner relies solely on location while tolerating unacceptable service standards and major disruptions to guests.
Lane cove national Park,pretty local parrot  will visit your site,great location

2025 Immer noch groß und sauber aber ungemütlich und teuer. Das Personal in der Rezeption war sehr unfreundlich, das liegt wohl an der Großstadt. Flugzeuglärm, vom  Internationalen Airport, ist leider auch oft zu hören. Der angrenzende Nationalpark ist das Große Plus für den Standort. Ansonsten nichts neues.

2019 Großzügiger Caravan Park in der Nähe vom Stadtzentrum Sydney. Direkt vom Park aus gibt es viele Wanderwege in den angrenzenden Nationalpark, wo man viele einheimische Tiere beobachten kann. Sanitäranlagen sind zum Teil alt aber sauber. Ansonsten ist alles vorhanden was man braucht. Ordentliche Anlage.

Der Platz ist eine Oase um Sydney zu erkunden. Perfekt angebunden an die Metro und Bus um in 30 min ins Zentrum zu fahren. Der Platz selber ist ruhig. Aber verglichen mit anderen australischen Plätzen vom Standard ein bis zwei Klassen darunter. Was mich stört sind unbefestigte Stellplätze an denen man den Tisch im Schlamm und Dreck aufstellt. Die Küche und Sozialräume sind akzeptabel. Es gibt zwar Strom und Wasser an den Stellplätzen, aber Wasser ist ein Gemeinschaftsanschluss. Leider kein Abwasser. Eine extreme Verbesserung wäre ein Grauwasseranschluss zum drüberfahren und ablassen. Auf dem gesamten Platz gibt es nur einen Dump Point. Ich glaube nicht das die Masse an Campern dahinfährt, den Schlauch auspackt und ablässt….

Staying at Discovery Parks Lane Cove is a highlight of our trips to Sydney. Being so close to the city, to walking trails, and to play areas and swimming pools, allows the grandchildren and us have the Best time when we are visiting. The new cabins and common areas are wonderful.

Just stayed for 2 nights in the Glamping cabins and they’re absolutely outstanding. The grounds are immaculate and the wildlife just made it an amazing experience. We now wished we’d stayed an extra couple of nights. If we ever come back to Sydney there’s no way we’d stay in the city again. The train to the city is a 10 minute walk from the camp and a 15 minute ride in. Loved it and would highly recommend. Mind you, we were in the brand new accommodation so I can only recommend that. The older cabins looked a little dated but as we weren’t in those I can’t say how they are. I’d also point out that we were warned that one of the women that works at reception wasn’t very friendly. I know who the reviews are referring to and I can tell you she was absolutely fine with us. Shame as I think the resorts reviews are being affected because of her.

Well maintained park. Very nice location, within walking distance of train stations and busses. Access to nice walks along the river too.
We requested specific sites due to special needs of family members and these were changed with no communication, even though I had called twice to confirm booking and enquire about early check (which they were very accommodating with). Overall a good stay, but could improve communication.

We stayed for one night in a motorhome site at this campground, and unfortunately our spot was located right next to the rubbish bins. The smell was extremely strong, and every time someone threw their trash away, the odour only got worse. I ended up spending the whole night with the smell of garbage right under my nose. They really should avoid placing sites so close to the bins.

Best caravan park in a national park. Nature at your doorstep. Kookaburra’s, Parrots, cockatoos n magpies everyday. Sites are easy to access anywhere in the park. There’s been a few new additions to the park which is great with Glamping tents and brand new cabins. I can’t comment on the insides as we have a caravan. Our favourite sites are 1-6.
Easy walking to the train to get to the city btw it’s a driverless train very cool.
Train can take you to Large Macquarie shopping centre getting off at Macquarie university stop n just walk to shops.
Theres Coles supermarket close by.
Take a walk to the river it’s easy and so many more animals you might see on your way.
I give this park 10/10
Borders the national park and it’s a beautiful spot with easy access to walks. Very peaceful location

This place was amazing. We stayed two nights (3 persons) with a 4WD and two roof tents. We didn't need power. We loved this place, so much wildlife in contact with the people in the park. Nearby (literally next to the park) is a national Park which is beautiful. Facilitys are all clean the staff is super friendly and it's quite although it's just 10 km from central Sydney. Sometimes you hear a plane but it's not that noisy. A little walk from the camping ground there is a metro station. With Bus it takes about 30 minutes to get directly into the center of Sydney. I just can repeat it was a very enjoyable time and stay. Definitely one of the best campsites in Sydney.

I always stay at this hidden gem while in Sydney for work. The bush location means great views and quiet setting for the cabins, away from the noise of Sydney while still being right in the middle of it.
The Metro station a few hundred metres away means the restaurants in Chatswood are just minutes down the line.
The cabins are well priced, clean and self contained. The newer deluxe cabins and the glamping cabins are downright fancy and still very good value compared to a hotel in Chatswood or North Ryde. After hours check in is never a problem, and transport options include the nearby Metro, buses or there are always plenty of Uber drivers within minutes.
Good spot for keeping a hire car too, it's an easy area to drive around compared to much of the city.
The grounds has a laundry, several amenities blocks, swimming pool and there are plenty of bush walks in the national park. Caravan sites are well spaced and well above average if you pick your spot carefully.

Try Narrabeen instead.

Rude receptionist who, on top of refusing any form of compensation for the two nights I couldn’t make it — not even a simple voucher for a future stay — felt the need to tell me “we’re not in Europe, we’re in Australia,” clearly because of my accent. I’ve lived in Australia for 14 years, so please don’t use the country as an excuse for poor customer service.
And honestly, how is someone speaking like this the face of a caravan park? It’s embarrassing for the business — and for the country.

On top of that, I spoke with the people on the site next to me, and apparently my campsite was double-booked while I was away. Some  table and chairs were still there when I returned on the third day of my four-day stay. Completely unacceptable.

I’ll be filing a formal complaint. And as for a voucher, don’t bother — you definitely won’t see me again.
Don't try and check in 10 minutes early because you will be told to come back in 10 minutes

Hebben hier gekampeerd met campervan. Prachtig en rustig gelegen bij National Park en op loopafstand van het treinstation North Ryde. Je zit zo in de stad. Tegenover het treinstation is tevens een Coles. Ongelofelijk in een stad als Sydney.
Goede warme douches, een zwembad,  buitencinema, bbq's en keuken.

Good campsites. Facilities not the newest and not always that clean.
From time to time you hear the planes from Sydney airport.
Staff could be more motivated.
There is a Metro Station close by to get into the city but it still takes you almost an hour.

Der Park liegt sehr zentral und man erreicht Sydney wirklich leicht - dabei behilflich sind Volunteers an der Bushaltestelle. Leider wissen die Parkbetreiber offensichtlich, dass Reisende den Park genau aus diesem Grund auswählen und legen daher keinen großen Wert auf Service, Freundlichkeit und Sauberkeit. Auch der nahegelegene Flughafen, der den Park zur Einflugsschneise macht, ist leider kein Pro. Ärgerlich für uns war, dass der Platz des Campers genau neben den Mülltonnen lag - das könnte man für Gäste etwas anders gestalten.
Very relaxing

Problems with late check-in. We stayed here overnight after our trip to Sydney. I had requested a late check-in in the notes box when booking. When we arrived at 8 p.m., we were standing in front of a mailbox and didn't have the code for it. I sent an urgent email to the park, which is still unanswered. Another visitor was able to help us by giving us the code. He said he had helped others before, so this is not an isolated case. There was also a letter with our name in the box. Incomprehensible and very stressful after a long day.
Nice and quiet.
So close to the metro

We have stayed at parks all over Australia and the majority have ovens and cooktops for the customers to use. Unfortunately, Discovery Lane Cove does not. The provided toaster and portable oven both don't work. Did tell staff when we were here months ago the first time, but it hadn't been rectified. Other than that, it is a perfect spot. It is quiet. A fair way off the main road and away from the highway. Easy drive to the restaurants and eateries in Lane Cove. Close to Macquarie Centre and Top Ryde should you need something bigger, and the train station is a 10 minute walk away. Would definitely stay here again.

Our family has stayed here three years in a row (2022-2024) during December (outside school holidays) in a cabin. We have enjoyed staying here with plenty of native birds, pool, and close by to the Metro. I went to check to make another booking and prices to stay in a cabin for a week have increased by 20%. This is an unreasonable increase in price.

We needed a cheap place near Sydney to stay the for the weekend, but we decided to spend just a little more than expected for a more fun experience and this was so worth it.

The cabin wasn’t flashy but it has everything you’d need, it was perfect and cozy with very effective heating. The only time we needed service it was quick and helpful, the caravan park itself is beautiful with 2 playgrounds, many amenities and countless kookaburras. The walking trails connected to the park were beautiful and refreshing and our main reason for booking, we rented a pedal boat (separately paid company, but accessible through the trail) down the river which was a beautiful experience, and it was insane to remember how short of a distance we were from the CBD.

The only con was the amount of mould on the bathroom ceiling, but even that I can understand would be hard to maintain. Overall we highly recommend!

Conveniently close to CBD.

On the positive side, the cockatoos and turkeys were fun to watch and definitely added some charm to the area.

Another downside was the lack of a baby bath in the toilet facilities, which was inconvenient. Even though there were only two cabins occupied during my stay, they didn’t offer us a late check-out, even though our flight wasn’t until 5 PM. *it is clean except but crockery and glasses need a check over

What an amazing caravan park right in the heart of Sydney surrounded by absolutely stunning National Park. Fantastic amenities, lovely sites and great staff. Also only a 10 minute stroll to the train station into for easy access into the city itself.   In particular a huge thanks to Sharon in reception for her friendliness and willingness to help us out when in need.  Really appreciated it!  Thank you so much,  Greg and Ellie
Great facilities and nearby bush walk along the river.very full unfortunately not alot of privacy and being on a flightpath no chance of sleeping in.planes were very loud and constant.
Stunning location amongst trees, birds & animals. Facilities are immaculately maintained, clean and the staff is awesome. Had a wonderful holiday thanks to the staff and will most definitely return.
Beutiful quiet Park.

4th time stayed at this place, due to the close to public transport, 10min walk to the tram station and public bus stop to the city or wherever you want to go.
The place is surrounded by nature, good walking tracks especially if you go down and turn left you will find good walking tracks but if you turn right, there will be quite a lot of picnic areas and boat hire shop

Kitchen has a large fridge and freezer to store your food
No more fridge and microwave in the bbq area near swimming pool
Few children playgrounds

Good water pressure in shower
WiFi signal is very weak
Our favourite place to stay in Sydney - easy to get to/from; nice and quiet; accommodation basic but everything you need; reasonably priced

Amazing piece of bushland in suburban Sydney. The cabins were clean and well appointed. The pool was nice to have in the warm weather. There was wildlife and birds visiting. A very pleasant place to stay while still being close to the Metro for access to everything. A great alternative to a hotel.
It’s an ideal place for caravan or R V

Beautiful park, relaxing,convenient and great staff. Checked in and was given a glorified parking spot facing the NP. Went back and politely asked if there was anything larger available and for how much. Olivia was fantastic and got us sorted out in less than five minutes. If our schedule allowed, would have def stayed an extra night or two!
Pool is clean and the kids jumping in and out seem very happy, but I can’t hear them from where our bus sits so I am very happy too 🥂

This caravan park is the perfect place to stay for a short holiday in Sydney. In the midst of a national park you can experience Australian bush and rain forest right next to Lane Cove River. Yet you are also in the middle of suburbia, with great restaurants and pubs close by. There are many hiking and bike trails. You are a short 10 minute walk to the nearest station. The facilities are great. For the adventurous or budget traveler or anyone looking for a different type of experience of Sydney.
Great place, nature on your doorstep, the city 5 minutes away and clean and quiet 🤫 👌
Just bad experience crowd place, noisy. Some one was having party and drinking till late night.  Never had such bad experience.

It is a great park, in a great location.

Having the main amenities closed for cleaning everyday between 7am and 9am is ridiculous.
The men's are closed for the first hour and not even being cleaned, the get closed whilst the ladies get cleaned.

This closure leads to queues at the smaller amenities, which only has the toilets.

Close it for cleaned 10am when most people have used the for the day.
Why do the cleaning at the busiest time of the day?

Very disappointed with our stay here. Considering we made a booking 12 months prior, we were put into a spot that was all covered in mud, no grass at all, and wasn’t even cleaned prior to arrival. No drainage on our site when it rained and all of the rain just collected on our site. When asked about what could be done, the workers said that other sites were booked out (when went for a walk during our stay and this was false). They were also quick to send a text message to indicate an arrival time, yet nothing was done on our site to be presentable. I will not be returning back to this caravan site and nor recommend this to any other camper. Don’t be fooled by the photos online.
Lovely grassy campsites with a pool, BBQs available. Cabins are a good size and the birds are very friendly along with many lizards running around

Awesome location, ok facilities. Our experience was below average. We had overflowing bins, full sanitary bins, and badly behaved groups around us, which really impacted our stay. If you can get a site backing up to the national park or the edges, you will have a more peaceful experience.
The speeding is out of control there, though, and they need to make changes. More signage, speed limit, and consequences in the handout provided, clear rules explained at check-in, staff, and contractors to set the standard (because they were not). We have been travelling for over 6m now full time, and this is the first time we haven't let our kids scoot or ride in a caravan park. It was just too dangerous.
I also didn't realise until several days later that there were other toilet blocks that staff could have suggested for me to see if the sanitary bins were empty. Or they could have called for an immediate service from the contractor. It's lucky I'm not the kind of person who puts things down the toilet.
If I was paying $30 per night, maybe these things would be tolerable. But at about $100 per night, I expect better.

Great location, walking distance to North Ryde train station.
Very basic amenities.
Disappointed I reported to reception that other campers where walking through our site yet they never send a message out to everyone to be respectful and walk around others sites.
Stayed 2 weeks and disappointed they never put 1 movie on. I asked if they showing a movie and they said no cos of rain.  Yet it never rained everyday. And they expected campers to camp in the rain.  Seems to me they to lazy to organise the same.
Sites where poorly maintained for what you pay.  Seen cartaker with a battery powered mower, which is not adequate for a such a large campsite.  I asked for grass to be cut on my site and he did after he had to charge the battery
Flight path! From 6am to 11pm, low level flights landing. Toilets cleaning 7-8am pretty stupid, and site not mown with a foot of grass (!!) attracted leeches in rain.

It could have been good camp ( good amenities) but they make it look like there is no site left unpowered and make you pay for powered.
Bad spirit

The caravan park looks amazing, but the cabins not so much. Cabins had heaps of ants, and the floors were very dirty. even after mopping.

The bunk-bed sheets are very dirty with other peoples hair and fluff which was there when we put the sheet on.

one of the woman workers were very rude.

The pool was dirty, the Turkeys doing their buisness right beside the pool, that took 3 days to get cleaned.

Great location but very dirty. Shopping centre just 3Kms away, and heaps of activities around.

Would only recommend to caravans.
Awesome 😎

We stayed here in September 2023, a great pick given location to Sydney, a Coles supermarket and access to road and rail options, all not too far away.

The park is located on the edge of Lane Cove National Park with a connecting pathway. We went exploring by car and Shanks's Pony. The bird life is magnificent.

Park staff are friendly and helpful. We asked to extend our stay by one night and they were able to do this without requiring us to move our van.
Right alongside wildlife, lorikeets, sulfer crest birds, bush turkeys right at your door. Take some cut-up fruit outside for the best up close interactions.

Fantastic accomodation for time spent in Sydney.  Don’t expect a beachside resort, as it doesn’t claim to be that and is not that, and no one will be disappointed.  But it is a very quiet and comfortable place to spend time in Sydney.

The park is wonderful, staff are friendly and amazingly helpful. Everything is well maintained and well priced. Even thou the weather want nice to us we had a wonderful stay :) thanks we will be back
Mitten in Sydney dieser wunderbare Campingplatz. Gleich am Nationalpark mit direktem Zugang. Sanitäre Anlagen waren gepflegt und sauber. Unser letzter Tag im Camper :(

Wir waren im November 2023 drei Nächte da mit einem Campervan. Ordentlicher und sauberer Platz, auch Toiletten, Duschen usw. Nach ca. 20 Minuten Fußweg gelang man zu Bus oder Bahn um in die Stadtmitte oder zum Hafen zu gelangen. Mit dem Campervan in die  Innenstadt zu fahren sollte man tunlichst vermeiden. Unerfreulich ist allerdings der Lärm der Flugzeuge, die alle paar Minuten tief über dem Campingplatz sich bis in die Nacht hinein im Landeanflug befinden.
Wonderful location in Sydney and very comfortable cabins. Staff were incredibly helpful in enabling us to stay an extra night. Will stay again.
Close to the city centre, easy and clean.
Loved the parrots, cockatoos and turkey visiting us

The only caravan and camping ground within range of Sydney CBD. Good value for money, nice setting, and good facilities.

Great find as a location from which to explore Sydney. Surprisingly quiet as it backs up on a National Park which has hiking trails and a river; however the Metro system is within walking distance.

Great location, lots of cute animals, we saw an echidna eating ants on one of our walks through the camping area. Great entertainment for the kids. Would have been nice if the camp kitchen didn't have open/close times, not the best for late night snacking.
Great place to stay and pretty close to most things around this awesome city
Fantastic bushland location within Sydney
My whole family had a great experience! Excellent customer service. Facilities are well-maintained. There are lots of outdoor activities to enjoy. We will definitely book again!
Great place for a short getaway but not too far fromhome

I have used 13 campsites in 3 weeks from Cairns to Sydney and this is the worst I have seen
Dirty, the equipment does not work, no product to clean the dishes, no tables to eat....
It looks like this campsite is abandoned.
The only positive point is that the campsite is close to the center of Sydney.

Stunning location in the middle of the city! It was like stepping into another world. So calm.

The park is lovely, as were the staff and manager.

There is a perfect spot for families to camp outside a kids play area, making it super convenient for adults and children to enjoy.

New bbqs and shared camp kitchens.
Love it. We stay here a lot. Beautiful location & close to everything.

A really enjoyable place. Planes overhead but not low down. Cemetery across the road was offset by wonderful sounds of nature coming from the woods.
Very close to rail network.

We loved this Park. Very green location in the National Park with well organised sites and facilities.
Even more impressive was the service of the very friendly staff, particularly Fiona and Stephania on reception, who went out of their way to help us with some problems we had on leaving.
Will be back for sure!!
Mick and Christine

Staff were lovely, especially the 2 grounds keepers , always a smile and a hello everytime they went past.
Kids enjoyed the little park and obstacle course/park.
Laundry facilities are eftpos friendly at the top laundry room, coin operated at the bottom one.
10mins down the road to Chatswood Mall and close to the motorway if you need to head out West .
Overall enjoyed the experience, and so did my family who checked in to the cabin next door.
Great park
Bardzo dobra miejscówka na kemping. Przystępna cenowo, dobrze skomunikowana z centrum (miejskie autobusy). Kuchnia jest świetnie wyposażona.

Cannot speak more highly of this Park. The staff are fabulous, from the ladies at reception, the cleaners and the groundsmen....a big shout out to Johno with the big smiley grin 😁.  We had some hiccups on our journey, my daughter was hospitalised for 7 days AND the engine in the 4wd had to be replaced but the staff here were so caring and accommodating. I was sad to leave.
Thank you Lane Cove C.P.
Highly recommend.
Awesome park, will be back
Love coming here to camp while in Sydney.  It's like being out in the bush and the convenience of the city all in 1.  Fantastic location and staff are always friendly throughout the park.
Great peaceful location amongst trees and parkland. Being Discovery Parks did not quite live up to usual Discovery standards.  Needs som me investment in fixing sites and kids playgrounds etc
Sharon at the front desk is always helpful and friendly! I’ve been staying at Lane Cove intermittently since May and it’s always a pleasure coming back! Thanks Sharon 🙏
Great setting and location. Poorly maintained park if you use facilities like the camp kitchen and laundry.
Quiet, good location, clean and friendly staff. That's all we need. You might even watch some parrots flying in out their tree nest if you are lucky.

We stayed here for a long weekend in winter in a powered site, fabulous location and super beautiful park.

Park itself is very well kept, it’s hard to believe that you are in the middle of Sydney.  With the new Metro line it’s around an hour to be in Circular Quay for Darling Harbour!  The park itself is full of gum trees and greenery; it really is a stunning park.

The run into the park from North or South is pretty clean with the vast majority of transit being on motorways.  We were nervous about being around Sydney with a large caravan and tow-rig but we’re pleasantly surprised.

Only negative from me is some of the sites are pretty tight for larger caravans, plus there is very low levels of light at night time.  I pulled in around 6:30pm and parking up my van felt pretty scetchy solo; would recommend doing this one in daytime hours!

Anyways, would happily stay here again!

A great spot near Sydney

3 night stay.  really convenient 40 minutes by bus from the city centre.  very neat and clean campsite.  pitches in grass and concrete, very clean bathrooms with laundry service and many services such as a BBQ area and kitchen area with everything you need.  it also has living areas and a cinema area, parks for children, a swimming pool and a gem;  a trekking route starts from the campsite which leads to the Lane Cove river.  $50 a night powered site.  Great
